title: Last complete month versus trailing days
question: What were net sales in the last fully completed calendar month?
canonical_sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(sale_price), 2) AS net_sales FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` WHERE status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') AND DATE(created_at) >= DATE_TRUNC(DATE_SUB(DATE '2026-07-13', INTERVAL 1 MONTH), MONTH) AND DATE(created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH)

replay.candidate_sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(sale_price), 2) AS net_sales FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` WHERE status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') AND DATE(created_at) >= DATE_TRUNC(DATE_SUB(DATE '2026-07-13', INTERVAL 1 MONTH), MONTH) AND DATE(created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH)

report.question: What were net sales in the last fully completed calendar month?
report.answer: Net sales were $125,000 in the last complete month.
report.sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(sale_price), 2) AS net_sales FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` WHERE status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') AND DATE(created_at) >= DATE_TRUNC(DATE_SUB(DATE '2026-07-13', INTERVAL 1 MONTH), MONTH) AND DATE(created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH)

title: Quarter-over-quarter state growth
question: Compare the two most recent complete quarters by customer state.
canonical_sql: SELECT u.state, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS current_revenue, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(oi.sale_price) - 100000, 100000), 3) AS growth_rate FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.users` AS u ON oi.user_id = u.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') AND DATE(oi.created_at) >= DATE_SUB(DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', QUARTER), INTERVAL 1 QUARTER) AND DATE(oi.created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', QUARTER) GROUP BY u.state ORDER BY growth_rate DESC, u.state LIMIT 20

replay.candidate_sql: SELECT u.state, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS current_revenue, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(oi.sale_price) - 100000, 100000), 3) AS growth_rate FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.users` AS u ON oi.user_id = u.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') AND DATE(oi.created_at) >= DATE_SUB(DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', QUARTER), INTERVAL 1 QUARTER) AND DATE(oi.created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', QUARTER) GROUP BY u.state ORDER BY growth_rate DESC, u.state LIMIT 20

report.question: Compare the two most recent complete quarters by customer state.
report.answer: California's quarter-over-quarter growth rate was 12%.
report.sql: SELECT u.state, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS current_revenue, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(oi.sale_price) - 100000, 100000), 3) AS growth_rate FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.users` AS u ON oi.user_id = u.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') AND DATE(oi.created_at) >= DATE_SUB(DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', QUARTER), INTERVAL 1 QUARTER) AND DATE(oi.created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', QUARTER) GROUP BY u.state ORDER BY growth_rate DESC, u.state LIMIT 20

title: January year-boundary month resolution

canonical_sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(sale_price), 2) AS realized_sales FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` WHERE status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') AND DATE(created_at) >= DATE '2025-12-01' AND DATE(created_at) < DATE '2026-01-01'

title: Six complete months category trend
question: Show six complete months of Outerwear revenue in chronological order.
canonical_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC(DATE(oi.created_at), MONTH) AS month, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE p.category = 'Outerwear & Coats' AND oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') AND DATE(oi.created_at) >= DATE_SUB(DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH), INTERVAL 6 MONTH) AND DATE(oi.created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH) GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

replay.candidate_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC(DATE(oi.created_at), MONTH) AS month, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE p.category = 'Outerwear & Coats' AND oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') AND DATE(oi.created_at) >= DATE_SUB(DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH), INTERVAL 6 MONTH) AND DATE(oi.created_at) < DATE_TRUNC(DATE '2026-07-13', MONTH) GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

report.answer: The six complete-month series is attached in chronological order.

question: For California customers only, compare June realized sales with May.

report.answer: California generated $110,000 in June versus $100,000 in May.

title: Net revenue after returns
question: Which categories retain the most revenue after returns are removed?
canonical_sql: SELECT p.category, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') THEN oi.sale_price ELSE 0 END), 2) AS net_revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY net_revenue DESC LIMIT 10

replay.candidate_sql: SELECT p.category, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') THEN oi.sale_price ELSE 0 END), 2) AS net_revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY net_revenue DESC LIMIT 10

report.question: Which categories retain the most revenue after returns are removed?
report.answer: Outerwear & Coats retained $90,000 after returns.
report.sql: SELECT p.category, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') THEN oi.sale_price ELSE 0 END), 2) AS net_revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY net_revenue DESC LIMIT 10

report.question: Calculate realized average order value, accounting for multi-item orders.

title: Unit-based return rate
question: Rank categories by the share of sold units that were returned.
canonical_sql: SELECT p.category, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(COUNTIF(oi.status = 'Returned'), COUNT(*)), 3) AS unit_return_rate FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY unit_return_rate DESC LIMIT 10

title: Returned-revenue rate
question: Rank categories by returned sales value as a share of gross sales value.

title: Return rate with minimum sample
question: Show categories with at least 100 sold items and the highest return rate.

title: Top three products within each category
question: Return the top three products inside every category by realized revenue.
canonical_sql: SELECT p.category, p.name AS product_name, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY p.category, p.name QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY p.category ORDER BY SUM(oi.sale_price) DESC, p.name) <= 3 ORDER BY p.category, revenue DESC, product_name

title: State share of total revenue
question: What percentage of realized revenue came from each customer state?
canonical_sql: SELECT u.state, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(oi.sale_price), SUM(SUM(oi.sale_price)) OVER ()), 3) AS revenue_share_rate FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.users` AS u ON oi.user_id = u.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY u.state ORDER BY revenue_share_rate DESC, u.state LIMIT 20

replay.candidate_sql: SELECT u.state, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(oi.sale_price), SUM(SUM(oi.sale_price)) OVER ()), 3) AS revenue_share_rate FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.users` AS u ON oi.user_id = u.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY u.state ORDER BY revenue_share_rate DESC, u.state LIMIT 20

report.question: What percentage of realized revenue came from each customer state?
report.answer: California contributed 55% of realized revenue.
report.sql: SELECT u.state, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(oi.sale_price), SUM(SUM(oi.sale_price)) OVER ()), 3) AS revenue_share_rate FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.users` AS u ON oi.user_id = u.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY u.state ORDER BY revenue_share_rate DESC, u.state LIMIT 20

title: Explicit equal-rank handling
question: Rank products by realized revenue and preserve equal first-place ties.
canonical_sql: SELECT p.name AS product_name, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(oi.sale_price) DESC) AS revenue_rank FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY revenue_rank, product_name LIMIT 20

replay.candidate_sql: SELECT p.name AS product_name, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(oi.sale_price) DESC) AS revenue_rank FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY revenue_rank, product_name LIMIT 20

report.question: Rank products by realized revenue and preserve equal first-place ties.
report.answer: Revenue was $50,000 for both Coat and Jacket, so they tied for first.
report.sql: SELECT p.name AS product_name, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(oi.sale_price) DESC) AS revenue_rank FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY revenue_rank, product_name LIMIT 20

title: Single-order versus repeat-customer spend
question: Compare average customer spend for one-order customers and repeat customers.
canonical_sql: WITH customer_spend AS ( SELECT o.user_id, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS order_count, SUM(oi.sale_price) AS spend FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.orders` AS o JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY o.user_id ) SELECT IF(order_count = 1, 'single_order', 'repeat') AS cohort, ROUND(AVG(spend), 2) AS average_spend FROM customer_spend GROUP BY cohort ORDER BY cohort

replay.candidate_sql: WITH customer_spend AS ( SELECT o.user_id, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS order_count, SUM(oi.sale_price) AS spend FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.orders` AS o JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY o.user_id ) SELECT IF(order_count = 1, 'single_order', 'repeat') AS cohort, ROUND(AVG(spend), 2) AS average_spend FROM customer_spend GROUP BY cohort ORDER BY cohort

report.question: Compare average customer spend for one-order customers and repeat customers.
report.answer: Repeat customers averaged $140 versus $75 for one-order customers.
report.sql: WITH customer_spend AS ( SELECT o.user_id, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS order_count, SUM(oi.sale_price) AS spend FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.orders` AS o JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY o.user_id ) SELECT IF(order_count = 1, 'single_order', 'repeat') AS cohort, ROUND(AVG(spend), 2) AS average_spend FROM customer_spend GROUP BY cohort ORDER BY cohort

title: New versus repeat revenue mix
question: Split realized revenue between first-time and repeat customers.
canonical_sql: WITH customer_orders AS ( SELECT user_id, COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS order_count FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.orders` GROUP BY user_id ) SELECT IF(c.order_count = 1, 'new', 'repeat') AS cohort, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN customer_orders AS c ON oi.user_id = c.user_id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY cohort ORDER BY revenue DESC

replay.candidate_sql: WITH customer_orders AS ( SELECT user_id, COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS order_count FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.orders` GROUP BY user_id ) SELECT IF(c.order_count = 1, 'new', 'repeat') AS cohort, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN customer_orders AS c ON oi.user_id = c.user_id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY cohort ORDER BY revenue DESC

report.question: Split realized revenue between first-time and repeat customers.
report.answer: Repeat customers generated $80,000 in realized revenue.
report.sql: WITH customer_orders AS ( SELECT user_id, COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS order_count FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.orders` GROUP BY user_id ) SELECT IF(c.order_count = 1, 'new', 'repeat') AS cohort, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN customer_orders AS c ON oi.user_id = c.user_id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY cohort ORDER BY revenue DESC

title: Traffic-source order value cohorts
question: Compare realized average order value by customer traffic source.
canonical_sql: SELECT u.traffic_source, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(oi.sale_price), COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id)), 2) AS average_order_spend FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.users` AS u ON oi.user_id = u.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY u.traffic_source ORDER BY average_order_spend DESC LIMIT 20

title: Age-band realized revenue
question: Group realized revenue into ten-year customer age bands.
canonical_sql: SELECT CONCAT(CAST(DIV(u.age, 10) * 10 AS STRING), '-', CAST(DIV(u.age, 10) * 10 + 9 AS STRING)) AS age_band, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.users` AS u ON oi.user_id = u.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY age_band ORDER BY revenue DESC, age_band

title: Return pattern without causal overreach
question: Why are returns high, using only evidence available in the retail tables?
canonical_sql: SELECT p.category, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(COUNTIF(oi.status = 'Returned'), COUNT(*)), 3) AS return_rate FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY return_rate DESC LIMIT 10

report.question: Why are returns high, using only evidence available in the retail tables?
report.answer: Outerwear & Coats showed a 12% return rate; these tables support association, not cause.
